prevalence.source: Ministério da Saúde / FEBRASGO guidance and obstetric epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; higher risk with increasing maternal age and obesity

burden.mortalityRate: Major contributor to maternal morbidity/mortality when severe
burden.source: IHME GBD / WHO maternal health estimates

vulnerablePopulations: pregnant people with limited prenatal care access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: New-onset hypertension after 20 weeks plus proteinuria and/or end-organ dysfunction; management per FEBRASGO and Ministério da Saúde obstetric guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: prenatal BP screening is routine in SUS; quality varies by region
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; outcomes depend on timely prenatal detection and access to referral maternity services

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Routine prenatal BP and urine protein screening in APS; severe cases referred to high-risk prenatal and maternity hospitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Aspirin prophylaxis in high-risk pregnancies, antihypertensives (labetalol/hydralazine/nifedipine), magnesium sulfate for seizure prophylaxis, and timely delivery planning
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Unequal access to high-risk obstetric care and ICU beds; delays in referral in rural/North regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Standardized prenatal protocols in SUS and broad availability of magnesium sulfate in maternity services
